title: Darth Desolous
content:

[[3522 BBY]]<ref name="birth date">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'' databank establishes that Darth Desolous was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] approximately 3500 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the beginning of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' provides the date of [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] for the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|opening battle]] of the conflict. Using simple math, Desolous' birth date can be approximated to the year [[3522 BBY]].</ref>
|death=[[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]]<ref name="TFU databank">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game]] {{C|[[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PSP]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PS2]] and [[Wikipedia:Wii|Wii]] versions' in-game databank}}

{{Quote|A thousand Jedi died cursing Darth Desolous. Now you too will scream my name.|Darth Desolous|<ref name="TFU" />}}
'''Darth Desolous''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Pau'an/Legends|Pau'an]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] prior to becoming a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. [[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] around [[3522 BBY]], he studied [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] teachings and fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], he was expelled from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. As a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Desolous swore revenge on the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and assembled an [[Darth Desolous's army|army]] of Pau'ans, whom he trained in [[Darth Desolous's martial art|martial arts]] and used in his [[Darth Desolous conflict|fight]] against the Order. Desolous and his forces [[Death/Legends|slew]] almost two thousand Jedi, until they were crushed by the Jedi Order during a [[Battle of Yaga Minor (Darth Desolous conflict)|battle]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]]. During the battle, Desolous was killed. A simulacrum of Desolous was later incorporated into the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple's]] [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trial of Skill]] program. In [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], the program activated itself and [[Starkiller's Trial of Skill|fought]] Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] secret [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]], and was defeated by him.

==Biography==
===The Master's fall===
{{Quote|It took the entire Council to kill me, boy. You have no hope.|Darth Desolous|<ref name="TFU" />}}
[[File:Darth_Desolous.jpg|thumb|right|Darth Desolous]]
The man who would eventually become [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Desolous was originally a revered [[Pau'an/Legends|Pau'an]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]<ref name="TFU databank" /> born around [[3522 BBY]].<ref name="birth date" /> A formidable warrior who craved battle, the Master studied [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] teachings and fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], which led to his expulsion from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Enraged, the individual became the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] "Darth Desolous" and vowed to have his revenge on the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].<ref name="TFU databank" /> Effectively, Desolous became the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] of that [[Time/Legends|time]] period.<ref name="TFU dark lord">In [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game]], during his [[Starkiller's Trial of Skill|duel]] with [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]], [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Desolous exclaims that he was considered to be the ruler of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] during his lifetime.</ref> Soon enough, Desolous began training an [[Darth Desolous's army|army]] of Pau'ans in his own [[Darth Desolous's martial art|style of martial arts]], which combined [[Cortosis shield/Legends|cortosis shields]] and melee weapons.<ref name="TFU databank" />

He eventually rallied his [[Army/Legends|army]] in a series of [[Darth Desolous conflict|ambushes]] against [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] that led to the [[Death/Legends|slaughter]] of nearly two thousand Jedi. With each victory Desolous grew more bold, but also more reckless and overconfident. Such carelessness ultimately led to his downfall, when Desolous and his army [[Battle of Yaga Minor (Darth Desolous conflict)|attacked]] a small force of Jedi on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]]. Those Jedi, however, were simply acting as bait meant to draw Desolous into a tactically disadvantageous position. When Desolous struck at Yaga Minor, a large Jedi [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] emerged from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and effectively prevented the Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] from retreating. Desolous's Pau'ans were outnumbered and eventually killed, and the Sith Lord himself was struck down as well,<ref name="TFU databank" /> although it allegedly required the entire [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] to do so.<ref name="TFU Jedi Council">The information about Desolous being [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] is derived from one of the Desolous simulacrum's random battle cries in [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game]]. That proclamation has not been supported or denied elsewhere.</ref>

===Legacy===
{{Quote|I am more powerful in death than I was in life!|Darth Desolous|<ref name="TFU" />}}
Darth Desolous's legacy far outlived his death. Although his birth name had been lost to history, his Sith name became synonymous with hatred and anger. Because of that, Desolous's visage was eventually incorporated into the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]] program for [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] who were considered for promotion to the rank of Jedi Knight.<ref name="TFU databank" /> The lifelike image of Desolous manifested as a personification of a Padawan's own personal weaknesses and, as part of the "Trial of Skill," was meant to help the students achieve control over their baseline raw emotions.<ref name="TFU databank trial of skill">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game]] {{C|[[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PSP]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PS2]] and [[Wikipedia:Wii|Wii]] versions' in-game databank}}
 '''Trial of Skill''' entry.</ref> Appearing in the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple's]] [[Jedi Trials chamber|trial chamber]], the simulacrum was fully aware of Desolous's physical death, and it boasted that it required the powers of the entire Jedi Council to kill him.<ref name="TFU" /> Even after the Jedi Temple was ravaged in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] during [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> the simulacrum of Desolous remained active, waiting for another opponent to engage in battle.<ref name="TFU" />

That [[Starkiller's Trial of Skill|battle]] finally occurred<ref name="TFU" /> around [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="TFUn">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' dates the [[Corellian Treaty]], an event that served as the formation of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] to [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. According to [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''The Force Unleashed'' novel]], the signing of the Corellian Treaty occurred roughly a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]] was nearly [[Death/Legends|killed]] aboard the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] by his [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. As the encounter with [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Desolous in [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game]] occurs before the events on the ''Executor'', the duel can be approximately dated to [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]].</ref> when Dark Lord of the Sith [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] sent his secret [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]], to the Jedi Temple to continue his [[Sith training|training]]. Once Starkiller entered the Trials Chamber, Desolous, armed with his signature [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Shield (physical armor)/Legends|shield]] and a [[Color/Legends|red]]-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], struck him with a bolt of red [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]. The two engaged in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]], with Desolous constantly [[Dun Möch/Legends|taunting]] Starkiller and bragging about the murders he had committed while he had been alive. However, Starkiller defeated the program when he threw the ancient Sith Lord on the chamber's floor and toppled a statue onto his body. Receiving new orders from Vader, Starkiller then left the Jedi Temple and embarked on a [[First mission to Raxus Prime|new mission]].<ref name="TFU" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Your hatred cannot defeat me.|Darth Desolous|<ref name="TFU" />}}
Darth Desolous was a gray-skinned Pau'an with black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="TFU" /> Before he fell to the dark side, Desolous was a respected Jedi Master. However, he was plagued by a lust for battle, which led to his fall. After his expulsion from the Jedi Order, Desolous became enraged and vengeful, remorselessly slaughtering numerous Jedi Knights in his bloodthirsty quest. With each killing, Desolous became increasingly overconfident, and in his arrogance, failed to perceive the Jedi trap on Yaga Minor until it was too late. Desolous' birth name was eventually forgotten, and his only legacy was one of hatred and rage.<ref name="TFU databank" />

Desolous' self-confidence was well represented by the Jedi Temple training program based on him. The simulation was evidently self-aware, believing that it was more powerful in death than Desolous had been in life, and voiced the intention to claim leadership of the Sith once again. The artificial manifestation admitted that Starkiller was powerful in the dark side, but still considered the apprentice no match for him.<ref name="TFU" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|The dark side is indeed your ally, but I am its master.|Darth Desolous|<ref name="TFU" />}}
[[File:Desolous_lightsaber.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Darth Desolous's lightsaber]]
Darth Desolous was a fierce warrior who developed his own fighting style that favored the usage of a cortosis shield along with a melee weapon. The shield was used to block an opponent's attack—even a lightsaber strike—leaving the enemy open to counterattack. Desolous was powerful enough to face and murder many Jedi,<ref name="TFU databank" /> and his simulacrum later claimed that the Sith Lord had been taken down only by the combined strength of the Jedi Council members.<ref name="TFU Jedi Council" /> The Desolous simulacrum employed a cortosis shield and a red-bladed lightsaber, and complemented their use with [[Force power/Legends|powers]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], including lightning, [[Force Enrage|rage]], and [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]].<ref name="TFU" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Darth Desolous was first mentioned on [[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]]'s blog in [[2006]], where his name was said to be inspired by the word "desolate."<ref name="Chee">{{Blog|holocron|28|Darth Names and the Darth Who winner revealed.}}</ref> No further information was revealed about him until [[2008]], which saw the release of [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''The Force Unleashed'' video game]]. Darth Desolous, appearing as a Jedi simulation, was featured in one of the Jedi Temple levels exclusive to the [[Krome Studios]]-developed [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PlayStation Portable]], and [[Wikipedia:Wii|Nintendo Wii]] versions of the [[Star Wars video games|game]], wherein he was voiced by [[Kristoffer Tabori]]. In those versions of ''The Force Unleashed'', entering "PAUAN" as a cheat code enables Darth Desolous as a playable character skin. Desolous is also an unlockable character in the PSP version's "Force Duel" mode.<ref name="TFU" />

The exact nature of Desolous's appearance in ''The Force Unleashed'' is rather ambiguous. However, the game's databank entry for Jedi Trials implies that the simulacrum is projected with the Force, stating that, "Some of the most common trials involved battles against fearsome Force constructs lurking deep within the bowels of the Jedi Temple."<ref name="TFU databank" /> On the other hand, [[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']], a [[2010]] reference book, describes a [[Holoprojector/Legends|holographic projector]] installed inside the Jedi Temple after the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|final battle]] on the planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] during the [[New Sith Wars]]. According to the book, the projector was used during the Jedi Trials by creating simulations of long-dead dark side "monsters."<ref name="The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref> The book's author, [[Daniel Wallace]], later confirmed in his blog that he intended this line to reference Darth Desolous and [[Darth Phobos]]—another Sith simulation from ''The Force Unleashed''—but he withheld from spelling their names out in order to avoid any potential timeline conflict.<ref name="Endnotes">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2010/12/endnotes-for-star-wars-jedi-path-part-3.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The Jedi Path (part 3 of 4)}}</ref>
